headline: NYSE fined $9 million by SEC over glitch that disrupted stock market
summary: NEW YORK, March 6 (Reuters) - The New York Stock Exchange has agreed to pay a $9 million ‌civil fine to settle U. Securities and Exchange ‌Commission charges over a computer glitch that disrupted the stock market open ​in January 2023, causing wild swings in the prices of blue-chip stocks.

headline: Are Oil Stocks Due for a Drop? What the Charts Say.
summary: Crude oil has rallied sharply in recent sessions with  jumping almost 25% for the week amid escalating Middle East tensions, as investors position for potential supply disruptions.  Strength in the energy sector was evident well before the recent escalations in the Middle East.  Demonstrating its consistency, it not fallen in consecutive sessions for two months.

headline: Oil Prices Soar Past $90. Kuwait Cut Production—and More Could Follow Suit.
summary: The price of  Brent crude  just surged past $90 a barrel for the first time in two years as the escalating conflict in Iran continues to fuel a meteoric rally that shows no signs of slowing down.  Brent crude futures  were trading at $91.11 a barrel on Friday, while  West Texas Intermediate futures  were at $88.96.  The move came after Kuwait was forced to reduce production at some of its oil facilities, according to a report from The Wall Street Journal.

headline: Exxon Mobil Reroutes Fuel And Revisits Venezuela As Risks And Options Shift
summary: Exxon Mobil is sending its first gasoline cargoes from the US Gulf Coast to Australia, responding to a global fuel crunch linked to a near halt of shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Company executives are also weighing a return to Venezuela, supported by improved heavy oil extraction technology. Together, these moves reflect a broad reworking of Exxon Mobil's global supply routes and upstream options as geopolitical tensions disrupt traditional flows. For investors tracking NYSE:XOM, these...

headline: What Exxon Mobil (XOM)'s Board Shift and Supply Rerouting Amid Gulf Tensions Means For Shareholders
summary: In February 2026, Exxon Mobil announced that director Jeffrey W. Ubben would not stand for re‑election at the May 27 annual meeting, while the company also began arranging gasoline and fuel shipments from the US Gulf Coast to Australia as Strait of Hormuz traffic was severely disrupted by the US‑Iran conflict. These moves highlight how Exxon Mobil’s governance changes and flexible global supply chains intersect with rising geopolitical risk and shifting oil flows tied to Middle East...
